---
title: "Replication: Beaudry and Portier (2014)"
subtitle: "News-Driven Business Cycles: Insights and Challenges"
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

## Overview

This document replicates the news shock identification of @beaudryportier2014. A news shock is identified as the shock that (i) has **no contemporaneous effect on TFP** and (ii) **maximizes the long-run response of TFP** at a 40-quarter horizon. The approach follows the mixed restriction strategy combining a zero impact restriction with a long-run maximization criterion.

## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup

library(tidyverse)
library(tictoc)
library(tidyMacro)

set_theme(ftheme_tidyMacro())
```

## Data

```{r}
#| label: data

# Variables: log TFP, log SP500, log Consumption, log Hours
y        <- BeaudryPortier2014 |> as.matrix()
T_obs    <- nrow(y)
N        <- ncol(y)
varnames <- colnames(y)
```

## VAR Estimation

```{r}
#| label: var-estimation

p <- 2
c <- 1

# Estimate VAR: y_t = c + A_1 y_{t-1} + ... + A_p y_{t-p} + e_t
var_result <- fVAR(y, p = p, c = c)

# Variance-covariance matrix of reduced-form residuals
Sigma <- var_result$sigma
```

## News Shock Identification

The news shock is identified as the shock that has zero impact on TFP but maximises the long-run response of TFP at horizon $H = 40$. Let $\Psi_h$ denote the $h$-step Wold coefficient matrices and $S$ the lower Cholesky factor of $\Sigma$. We search for a unit vector $\mathbf{h}$ such that the structural impact vector is:

$$
\mathbf{b} = S \begin{pmatrix} 0 \\ \mathbf{h}/\|\mathbf{h}\| \end{pmatrix}
$$

and the last-horizon response of TFP, $e_1^\top \Psi_H \mathbf{b}$, is maximized.

```{r}
#| label: identification

horizon <- 40

# Wold IRFs by inverting (I - A(L))
wold <- fwoldIRF(var_result, horizon = horizon)

# Lower Cholesky factor of Sigma
S <- t(chol(Sigma))

# LR-Max point estimate: closed-form solution, sign-normalised
struct_irf <- fmaxIRF(wold, S, var_idx = 1L)
```


## Bootstrap Confidence Bands: LR-Max Identification

```{r}
#| label: bootstrap-max

tic()
boot_max <- fbootstrapMaxCorrected(
  y          = y,
  var_result = var_result,
  nboot1     = 2000,
  nboot2     = 4000,
  horizon    = horizon,
  var_idx    = 1,          # maximise long-run effect on TFP (variable 1)
  cumulate   = integer(0), # no cumulation (levels data)
  prc        = 68,
  prc2       = 68
)
toc()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-irf-boot-max
#| fig-cap: "IRF to news shock with 90%/68% LR-Max bootstrap bands"

fplotirfLR(
  point       = struct_irf,
  boot_result = boot_max,
  varnames    = varnames,
  facet_ncol  = 2
)
```

## Bootstrap Confidence Bands: Uhlig Max-Share Identification

```{r}
#| label: bootstrap-uhlig

# Point estimate: FEV-maximising h2, sign-normalised
struct_irf_uhlig <- fuhligIRF(wold, S, idx = 1L)

tic()
boot_uhlig <- fbootstrapUhligCorrected(
  y          = y,
  var_result = var_result,
  nboot1     = 2000,
  nboot2     = 4000,
  horizon    = horizon,
  idx        = 1L,        # maximise FEV share of TFP (variable 1)
  cumulate   = integer(0),
  prc        = 68 # Match original study
)
toc()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-irf-boot-uhlig
#| fig-cap: "IRF to news shock with 90%/68% Uhlig max-share bootstrap bands"

fplotirfLR(
  point       = struct_irf_uhlig,
  boot_result = boot_uhlig,
  varnames    = varnames,
  facet_ncol  = 2
)
```
